stock_mentions.reason_mentioned: Currency devaluation and surprise rate hike, signaling economic reform and potential IMF support, which is positive for sovereign credit despite initial insufficiency for local assets.
stock_mentions.original_sentences: Egypt Deval: Not yet enough for local assets, but a positive sign for credit.The latest devaluation has only brought the Egyptian Pound close to spot fair value from overvalued territory, and additional valuation buffers and/or higher interest rates are needed to rebuild an attractive undervalued carry story — given both the deteriorating inflation outlook and the stiff competition from other EM FX carry trade opportunities.Nevertheless, the currency devaluation is a signal of a move towards economic reform, and likely IMF support and collaboration. This should eventually be supportive for sovereign credit, similar to the post-2016 experience and the historical patterns of countries entering an IMF programme.
stock_mentions.source_file: The EM Trader_ The Local Rates landscape after the Russia_Ukraine shock.pdf

stock_mentions.reason_mentioned: FX bond payments, uncertainty around willingness to pay, high probability of sovereign default still priced in, and large drawdowns in equities.
stock_mentions.original_sentences: Two of Russia’s FX bond payments down, more to go; EM HY credit issuance: Market access is costly but open in March; An unwind of risk premium in CEE assets, inflation risk and higher rates should continue to buffer the FX; EM equity funds faced significant performance pressure in February.Russia paid the $117mn in interest payments which were due on its USD bonds last week.Russia’s CDS and USD bond spreads tightened by ~2000bp on the initial news of the payment, but with the 5-year CDS still at ~1500bp, and 5-year cash bonds below ~30 cents on the USD, the market is still pricing a high probability of a sovereign default (a roughly two-thirds probability assuming a recovery rate of 25%).The ‘double-punch’ of large drawdowns in both Russian and Mainland Chinese equities have wrought significant pressure on EM equities this year.
stock_mentions.source_file: The EM Trader_ The Local Rates landscape after the Russia_Ukraine shock.pdf

stock_mentions.reason_mentioned: Coupon payment blocked for compliance reasons by Société Générale New York, highlighting near-term uncertainty for Russian corporates regarding their USD bond liabilities.
stock_mentions.original_sentences: Similar to the sovereign, Russia’s corporates have so far also been able and willing to meet their USD bond liabilities, but the near-term uncertainty is also high for this part of the market, which has ~$5bn due in the coming months, as evidenced by the coupon payment by Evraz on 21 March, which the company stated was blocked for compliance reasons by Société Generale New York.
stock_mentions.source_file: The EM Trader_ The Local Rates landscape after the Russia_Ukraine shock.pdf

stock_mentions.reason_mentioned: Came to market with large concessions for high-yield issuance, indicating unfavorable market conditions despite being an oil exporter.
stock_mentions.original_sentences: Volatility remains high, and this trend has continued into March, with Nigeria and Turkey coming to the market with large concessions, taking HY issuance so far in March to ~$3.25bn.Zooming in on Nigeria, we show how the new bond priced in comparison to the secondary yield curve on the day prior to issuance in Exhibit 18. Here we find that a large concession was offered, with the coupon for the new 7-year bond priced over 50bp higher than the yield of the ~8-year bond the day prior to issuance.

stock_mentions.reason_mentioned: Correspondent and payable-through account sanctions by the US, effectively preventing Dollar transactions, as part of stronger measures against Russia's largest financial institutions.
stock_mentions.original_sentences: Specifically, the Biden administration announced action against Russia’s two largest financial institutions, Sberbank and VTB. It is imposing correspondent and payable-through account sanctions on Sberbank, and full-blocking sanctions on VTB. Sberbank and VTB combined hold 60% of all Russian deposits and over half of all Russian wages and pensions are paid through Sberbank. Following Russia’s escalation on February 23, on February 24 the US sanctioned five additional banks, including Russia’s largest financial institutions Sberbank and VTB. Sberbank will be prohibited from maintaining correspondent accounts at US institutions, effectively preventing Dollar transactions. The US applied financing prohibitions (new debt longer than 14 days and new equity) against 13 firms – two private entities and 11 SOEs, including Sberbank, Gazprombank, and Gazprom.
stock_mentions.source_file: Top of Mind_ Russia Risk .pdf
